description: Subject's stance on whether moral obligations attach primarily to the individual (rights, autonomy) or to the collective (group harmony, shared responsibility).
Subject's stance on whether moral rules apply universally or whether obligations to family, community, or group take precedence over rules.

description: Subject's stance on whether human flourishing comes from harmony with the existing order (nature, social structure) or from active mastery and reshaping of one's environment.
Subject's stance on whether moral and practical reasoning should privilege near-term concerns or long-horizon future consequences.

description: Subject's stance on whether the natural world holds intrinsic moral significance or is primarily a resource for human use.

description: Subject's stance on whether preserving social face and harmonious relationships is more important than direct, frank communication, when the two conflict.
